revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

{{神社| 名称=手力雄神社| 画像=| 所在地=館山市大井1129| 祭神=[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]]| 社格=| 創建=伝 神武天皇元年| 本殿=| 例祭=10月9日| 神事=| }} '''手力雄神社'''（たぢからおじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[館山市]]にある[[神社]]。
==歴史== 伝承によれば、[[神武天皇]]元年（[[紀元前660年]]）に[[忌部氏]]が祀ったことに始まるという。[[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）には[[里美義頼]]の寄進により社殿が改築された。江戸時代までは大井大明神と呼ばれ、[[里見氏]]、次いで[[江戸幕府]]からも崇敬を受け、社領を安堵されている。
==主な神事== *9月 [[敬老の日]]の前の土曜日曜 安房国司祭（八幡の祭、やーたんまっち） *10月9日 例大祭 安房国司祭は[[鶴谷八幡宮]]（旧安房国総社）の祭で、当社からは神輿1台が参加する。
==文化財== *本殿 千葉県の有形文化財 **[[元禄大地震]]（[[1703年]]）で損壊したのを[[宝永]]年間（[[1704年]]～[[1710年]]）に修築したもの。[[安土桃山時代]]の様式が蟇股や垂木の反りなど随所にみられ、また一部には江戸中期の特徴もあらわれている（[http://furusato.awa.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 参照]）。 *大杉 館山市の天然記念物
==交通アクセス== *[[館山日東バス]]／[[鴨川日東バス]] [[館山駅]]～[[鴨川駅]] 乗車、「九重大井」下車。
==関連項目== *[[鶴谷八幡宮]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 八幡の祭]
{{神道 横}} [[Category:千葉県の神社|たちからおしんしや]] [[Category:館山市|たちからおしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

==文化財== *本殿 千葉県の有形文化財 **[[元禄大地震]]（[[1703年]]）で損壊したのを[[宝永]]年間（[[1704年]]～[[1710年]]）に修築したもの。[[安土桃山時代]]の様式が蟇股や垂木の反りなど随所にみられ、また一部には江戸中期の特徴もあらわれている（[http://furusato.awa.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 参照]）。 *大杉 館山市の天然記念物

{{出典の明記|date=2020年8月22日 (土) 09:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=手力雄神社 |画像=[[File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg|280px|手力雄神社・拝殿]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|frame-width=250|marker=place-of-worship}} |所在地=千葉県館山市大井1129 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|0|8.80|N|139|56|7.82|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=伝 神武天皇元年 |本殿= |例祭=10月9日 |神事= |}} '''手力雄神社'''（たぢからおじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[館山市]]にある[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 伝承によれば、[[神武天皇]]元年（[[紀元前660年]]）に[[忌部氏]]が祀ったことに始まるという。[[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）には[[里見義頼]]の寄進により社殿が改築された。江戸時代までは大井大明神と呼ばれ、[[里見氏]]、次いで[[江戸幕府]]からも崇敬を受け、社領を安堵されている。
== 主な神事 == * 9月 [[敬老の日]]の前の土曜日曜 安房国司祭（八幡の祭、やーたんまっち） * 10月9日 例大祭 安房国司祭は[[鶴谷八幡宮]]（旧安房国総社）の祭で、当社からは神輿1台が参加する。
== 文化財 == * 本殿 [[千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|千葉県指定有形文化財（建造物）]] ** 三間社流造。天正12年（1584年）の棟札あり。[[元禄大地震]]（[[1703年]]）で損壊したのを[[宝永]]年間（[[1704年]]～[[1710年]]）に修築したもの。[[安土桃山時代]]の様式が蟇股<!--や垂木の反り-->など随所にみられ、また一部には江戸中期の特徴もあらわれている（[http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 参照]）。 * 大杉 [[館山市指定文化財一覧|館山市指定天然記念物]] [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|手力雄神社・鳥居]]
== 交通アクセス == * [[館山日東バス]]／[[鴨川日東バス]] [[館山駅]]～[[安房鴨川駅|鴨川駅]] 乗車、「九重大井」下車。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[鶴谷八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 八幡の祭]
{{神道 横}}
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たちからおしんしや たてやま}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:館山市の歴史]] [[Category:館山市の建築物]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
<!--Tajikarao Shrine (Tateyama City)-->
{{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Shrine |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg|280px|Hand Hall of Tajikarao Shrine]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|frame-width=250|marker=place-of-worship}} |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|8.80|N|139|56|7.82|E|scale:5000}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BCE|ja|紀元前660th year }}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|ja|1584th year }}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|ja|1584th year }}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震}} ({{ill|1703|ja|1703rd year }}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}} ({{ill|1704|ja|1704th year }}–{{ill|1710|ja|1710th year }}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス}}: Board at {{ill|Tateyama Station|ja|館山駅}} or {{ill|Awa-Kamogawa Station|ja|安房鴨川駅}}; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮}}
== External Links == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 Yawata Festival Information]

revision.text:
<!--Tajikarao Shrine (Tateyama City)-->
{{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Shrine |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg|280px|Hand Hall of Tajikarao Shrine]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|frame-width=250|marker=place-of-worship}} |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|8.80|N|139|56|7.82|E|scale:5000}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|12=draft}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BCE|ja|紀元前660th year|12=draft}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1584|ja|1584th year|12=draft}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|12=draft}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|12=draft}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=draft}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=draft}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1584|ja|1584th year|12=draft}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=draft}} ({{ill|1703|ja|1703rd year|12=draft}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}} ({{ill|1704|ja|1704th year|12=draft}}–{{ill|1710|ja|1710th year|12=draft}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=draft}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=draft}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=draft}}: Board at {{ill|Tateyama Station|ja|館山駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Awa-Kamogawa Station|ja|安房鴨川駅|12=draft}}; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 Yawata Festival Information]
{{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}

Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=draft}} ({{ill|1703|ja|1703rd year|12=draft}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}} ({{ill|1704|ja|1704th year|12=draft}}–{{ill|1710|ja|1710th year|12=draft}}). revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年8月22日 (土) 09:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=手力雄神社 |画像=[[File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg|280px|手力雄神社・拝殿]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|frame-width=250|marker=place-of-worship}} |所在地=千葉県館山市大井1129 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|0|8.80|N|139|56|7.82|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=伝 神武天皇元年 |本殿= |例祭=10月9日 |神事= |}} '''手力雄神社'''（たぢからおじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[館山市]]にある[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 伝承によれば、[[神武天皇]]元年（[[紀元前660年]]）に[[忌部氏]]が祀ったことに始まるという。[[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）には[[里見義頼]]の寄進により社殿が改築された。江戸時代までは大井大明神と呼ばれ、[[里見氏]]、次いで[[江戸幕府]]からも崇敬を受け、社領を安堵されている。
== 主な神事 == * 9月 [[敬老の日]]の前の土曜日曜 安房国司祭（八幡の祭、やわたんまち） * 10月9日 例大祭 安房国司祭は[[鶴谷八幡宮]]（旧安房国総社）の祭で、当社からは神輿が参加する。
== 文化財 == * 本殿 [[千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|千葉県指定有形文化財（建造物）]] ** 三間社流造。天正12年（1584年）の棟札あり。[[元禄大地震]]（[[1703年]]）で損壊したのを[[宝永]]年間（[[1704年]]～[[1710年]]）に修築したもの。[[安土桃山時代]]の様式が蟇股<!--や垂木の反り-->など随所にみられ、また一部には江戸中期の特徴もあらわれている（[http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 参照]）。 * 大杉 [[館山市指定文化財一覧|館山市指定天然記念物]] [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|手力雄神社・鳥居]]
== 交通アクセス == * [[館山日東バス]]／[[鴨川日東バス]] [[館山駅]]～[[安房鴨川駅|鴨川駅]] 乗車、「九重大井」下車。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[鶴谷八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 八幡の祭]
{{神道 横}}
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たちからおしんしや たてやま}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:館山市の歴史]] [[Category:館山市の建築物]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]]

'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].

== History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|qq=|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.

== Major Rituals ==
* September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival")
* October 9 - Annual Grand Festival
The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.

== Cultural Properties ==
* **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture)
** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source])

'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].

== History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|qq=|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.

== Major Rituals ==
* September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival")
* October 9 - Annual Grand Festival
The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.

== Cultural Properties ==
* **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture)
** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source])
* **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City)
[[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

== Transportation ==
* {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|qq=|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|qq=|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at [[:en:Tateyama Station (Chiba)|Tateyama Station]] or [[:en:Awa-Kamogawa Station|Awa-Kamogawa Station]]; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11496924]] Wikidata name: Tajikarao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00244444444444|N|139.93541666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Tateyama|WD=Q646755}} }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at [[:en:Tateyama Station (Chiba)|Tateyama Station]] or [[:en:Awa-Kamogawa Station|Awa-Kamogawa Station]]; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 Yawata Festival Information]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11496924]] Wikidata name: Tajikarao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00244444444444|N|139.93541666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Tateyama|WD=Q646755}} }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at [[:en:Tateyama Station (Chiba)|Tateyama Station]] or [[:en:Awa-Kamogawa Station|Awa-Kamogawa Station]]; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 Yawata Festival Information]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496924]] Wikidata name: Tajikarao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00244444444444|N|139.93541666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Tateyama|WD=Q646755}} }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at [[:en:Tateyama Station (Chiba)|Tateyama Station]] or [[:en:Awa-Kamogawa Station|Awa-Kamogawa Station]]; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}

'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]].
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ([[:en:660 BC|660 BCE]]) by the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]]. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]] and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]]: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 12 ([[:en:1584|1584]]) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ([[:en:1703|1703]]) and reconstructed during the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]] ([[:en:1704|1704]]–[[:en:1710|1710]]). Features architectural styles from the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tajikarao-jinja (Tateyama, Chiba)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (館山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496924]] Wikidata name: Tajikarao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00244444444444|N|139.93541666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Tateyama|WD=Q646755}} }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba|en|Tateyama, Chiba|WD=Q646755|lt=Tateyama City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BC|en|660 BC|WD=Q1059526|lt=660 BCE}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|WD=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at {{ill|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|en|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|WD=Q553883|lt=Tateyama Station}} or {{ill|Awa-Kamogawa Station|en|Awa-Kamogawa Station|WD=Q552842|lt=Awa-Kamogawa Station}}; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www2.city.tateyama.chiba.jp/Guide/?tpcid=44&stoid=428 Yawata Festival Information]

'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba|en|Tateyama, Chiba|WD=Q646755|lt=Tateyama City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BC|en|660 BC|WD=Q1059526|lt=660 BCE}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BC|en|660 BC|WD=Q1059526|lt=660 BCE}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|WD=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at {{ill|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|en|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|WD=Q553883|lt=Tateyama Station}} or {{ill|Awa-Kamogawa Station|en|Awa-Kamogawa Station|WD=Q552842|lt=Awa-Kamogawa Station}}; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}

'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba|en|Tateyama, Chiba|WD=Q646755|lt=Tateyama City|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BC|en|660 BC|WD=Q1059526|lt=660 BCE|1=660 BC}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan|1=Inbe clan}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|WD=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan|1=Satomi clan}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=Q11406175|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704|1=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704|1=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]

{{Citation-needed|date=August 22, 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tajikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:手力雄神社・拝殿.jpg |location = Chiba Prefecture, Tateyama City, Ōi 1129 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00244444444444|N|139.93541666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |Rank = Gōsha (Local Shrine) |consecration_year = Said to be the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu |Main Hall = |festival = October 9 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Tateyama|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}} }}
'''Tajikarao Shrine''' (たぢからおじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba|en|Tateyama, Chiba|WD=Q646755|lt=Tateyama City|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was first established in the 1st year of Emperor Jimmu ({{ill|660 BC|en|660 BC|WD=Q1059526|lt=660 BCE|1=660 BC}}) by the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan|1=Inbe clan}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}), the shrine building was reconstructed with contributions from {{ill|Satomi Yoshiyori|ja|里見義頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshiyori|1=Satomi Yoshiyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644669}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ōi Daimyōjin and was revered by the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|WD=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan|1=Satomi clan}} and later by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was granted protected shrine lands.
== Major Rituals == * September - Saturday and Sunday before {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}}: Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri (Yawata no Matsuri, "Yawata Festival") * October 9 - Annual Grand Festival The Awa Kuni no Miyatsukomatsuri is the festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴谷八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the former provincial shrine of Awa Province, where a mikoshi (portable shrine) from this shrine participates.
== Cultural Properties == * **Main Hall** - {{ill|List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tangible Cultural Properties of Chiba Prefecture|1=Q11406175|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}} (Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture) ** Constructed in the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}) as indicated by the ridge tag. Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704|1=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
== Transportation == * {{ill|Tateyama Nitto Bus|ja|館山日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Nitto Bus|1=Tateyama Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666934}} / {{ill|Kamogawa Nitto Bus|ja|鴨川日東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa Nitto Bus|1=Kamogawa Nitto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675558}}: Board at {{ill|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|en|Tateyama Station (Chiba)|WD=Q553883|lt=Tateyama Station|1=Tateyama Station (Chiba)}} or {{ill|Awa-Kamogawa Station|en|Awa-Kamogawa Station|WD=Q552842|lt=Awa-Kamogawa Station|1=Awa-Kamogawa Station}}; alight at "Kyūdai Ōi."

Damaged in the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q770728}} ({{ill|1703|en|1703|WD=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}}) and reconstructed during the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1704|en|1704|WD=Q6824|lt=1704|1=1704}}–{{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}). Features architectural styles from the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, such as kaerumata (frog-leg struts) and eaves curvature, alongside elements characteristic of the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. ([http://furusato.mbit.or.jp/modules/dbx/?op=story&storyid=610 Source]) * **Great Cedar Tree** - {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja|館山市指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Natural Monuments of Tateyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Natural Monument of Tateyama City) [[File:手力雄神社・鳥居.jpg|thumb|Tajikarao Shrine Torii Gate]]
